questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 4 cm × 5 cm × 6 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height. V = 4 × 5 × 6 = 120 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has radius 3 cm and height 10 cm. What is its volume? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = 3.14 × 3² × 10 = 3.14 × 9 × 10 = 282.6 cm³.

questions.question: A cone has radius 5 m and height 12 m. Find its volume. (Use π ≈ 3.14)
A sphere has radius 2 inches. What is its volume? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 15 cm² and height 8 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height. V = 15 × 8 = 120 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has a volume of 250π cm³ and radius 5 cm. What is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²h, so 250π = π(5²)h, which gives 250π = 25πh. Dividing both sides by 25π yields h = 10 cm.
